assessment: This is a functional but under-designed reference catalogue. The table renders strain name, a color-coded type badge, and description in a clear 3-column layout with an obvious search and a single prominent 'Create Strain' CTA. But for an ERP 'Products & Strains' screen, this page shockingly under-delivers: there is no column for product count, SKU count, stock/availability, last-updated, or any numeric context; descriptions are lorem-ipsum filler eating 50% of the horizontal space; there is no filter by Type (Indica/Sativa/Hybrid) despite the badge being the second column; and each row has no affordance for the 'edit strain' action promised by the baseline. Users browsing to pick a strain for an order get zero operational signal from this page.
notable_strengths: Clear H2 + subtitle pattern ('Products & Strains' / 'Manage strains and product varieties') with a single, unambiguous primary action.
Color-coded Type badges (Hybrid/Indica/Sativa) give instant visual categorization while scanning.
Search input is large, prominent, and sits directly above the table — matches `useTableUrlState` URL-state behavior.

findings.title: Row has no visible edit affordance or row action
findings.what_is_wrong: The baseline lists 'edit strain' as a feature, but rows (Apple Fritter, Biscotti, Blue Dream, etc.) show no pencil icon, kebab menu, hover action, or clickable name. There's no way to discover how to edit a strain from this table.
findings.why_it_matters: Admins maintaining the catalogue hit a dead-end — the only visible write action is 'Create Strain', so editing an existing strain is undiscoverable and requires guessing (click the row? the name?).
findings.proposed_fix: Add a trailing Actions column with a kebab menu per row ('Edit strain', 'View products', 'Archive') and make the strain name a clickable link that opens the same edit dialog. Show the kebab at low opacity by default, full opacity on row hover.
findings.functionality_preserved: Uses the existing `strains.update` tRPC mutation and the edit strain dialog from the baseline — this is purely surfacing an existing feature, no behavior change.

findings.title: No Type filter despite Type being a first-class column
findings.what_is_wrong: Type (Indica/Sativa/Hybrid) is rendered as a badge column but the toolbar offers only a free-text search. There is no chip filter, segmented control, or dropdown to narrow by category.
findings.why_it_matters: A catalogue of dozens of strains is the exact case where category filtering matters most; users currently have to scroll and visually scan badges. This slows every 'find me all Indicas' task by well over 30%.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a segmented filter to the right of the search input: 'All | Indica | Sativa | Hybrid', persisted to URL state alongside the existing search param.
findings.functionality_preserved: Extends the existing `useTableUrlState`-driven search by adding a `type` query param consumed by `strains.list`; the search input and its URL-state behavior are untouched.

findings.title: Table carries no operational signal — just name, badge, and filler description
findings.what_is_wrong: Three columns: Strain Name, Type, Description. Description is the widest column and is pure lorem-ipsum on staging. There is no product/SKU count, no 'last updated', no indicator of whether the strain is in active use.
findings.why_it_matters: On a wholesale ERP, a catalogue page that can't tell you how many SKUs map to a strain — or which strains are orphaned — is decorative rather than operational. Admins can't triage stale entries.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace the Description column width with two new numeric columns from `productCatalogue.*`: '# Products' (right-aligned) and 'Last updated' (relative time). Truncate Description to a single line at ~40ch with a tooltip for the full text.
findings.functionality_preserved: Description remains visible (truncated with tooltip) and the new columns are read-only projections over existing `products.*` / `productCatalogue.*` routers — no schema or mutation changes.

redesign_sketch: Header: H1 'Products & Strains' with subtitle unchanged, 'Create Strain' stays top-right. Toolbar row: left — search input (unchanged width); right — segmented Type filter [All | Indica | Sativa | Hybrid], then a small 'Refresh' icon button. Table columns: Strain Name (clickable, opens edit dialog) | Type (badge) | Products (right-aligned count, links to filtered products view) | Description (single-line truncate, tooltip on hover) | Last updated (relative) | ⋯ kebab (Edit, View products, Archive). Row hover reveals kebab at full opacity and highlights the row. Empty state inside the table when search returns nothing: 'No strains match "{query}" — Clear search or Create strain.' All URL state continues flowing through `useTableUrlState`.
